initial_screening: I enjoy working close to hardware rather than only writing code.
I prefer decisions based on data and logic rather than intuition.
I am curious about how software or apps work behind the scenes.
I enjoy analyzing how mechanical systems behave under changing conditions.
I enjoy learning new technologies on my own.
I like understanding how machines or physical systems operate.
I am interested in how databases store and retrieve large amounts of data.
I prefer structured problem statements over open-ended creative tasks.
I enjoy understanding how noise affects signal quality.
I am comfortable sitting for long hours solving problems on a computer.
adaptive_pool: I enjoy working with circuits, electronic components, or hardware kits.
I enjoy breaking complex problems into smaller, logical steps.
I am curious about how electricity is generated, transmitted, and distributed.
I enjoy designing layouts, visuals, or user experiences.
I am interested in how signals, networks, or communication systems work.
I enjoy analyzing voltage, current, or power relationships in electrical systems.
I like puzzles, coding challenges, or logic-based games.
I enjoy thinking about motion, forces, or energy transfer in physical systems.
I enjoy integrating different software tools or platforms together.
I remain focused and motivated even when solving a problem takes a long time to show results.
I am comfortable following strict standards, specifications, or safety rules.
I am interested in how information is encoded and decoded for transmission.
I prefer practical, hands-on work over theoretical study.
I enjoy reasoning about correctness of algorithms, not just output.
I enjoy seeing real-world physical output rather than only screen-based results.
I am curious about how materials behave under stress, heat, or pressure.
I like identifying trade-offs between efficiency, cost, and performance.
I enjoy configuring or managing software-based systems.
I enjoy understanding how large systems work as a whole, not just individual parts.
I enjoy thinking about system efficiency beyond just writing code.
I am interested in how software performance scales with input size.
I enjoy working with systems where safety margins are critical.
I enjoy understanding how electrical or electronic systems behave under different conditions.
I am curious about how electrical faults are detected and corrected.
I enjoy working on long-term projects that require repeated testing and refinement.
I enjoy understanding how communication systems maintain reliability.
I enjoy debugging mistakes until I find the exact cause.
I am curious how small differences in machine parts can change how a machine works.
I enjoy thinking about failure modes in mechanical designs.
I enjoy analyzing problems related to power, load, or energy usage.
